title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot observes that the garden is meticulously maintained, suggesting that Mrs. Dreyfus may be a hindrance to Michael Garfield. Following a church committee meeting, Poirot speaks with Mrs. Royana Dreyer, a domineering woman. Poirot mentions that Miss Elizabeth Whitaker, a math teacher at Oakwood School, suggested Mrs. Dreyer might have witnessed something significant during the recent party.

Poirot inquires about a vase full of flowers. Mrs. Dreyer recalls that she found a glass vase containing wilted autumn leaves and chrysanthemums on a staircase table without water. She took it to the bathroom to fill it, but found no one there. She then explains that she accidentally dropped and shattered the wedding present in the hall. Miss Whitaker assisted her in sweeping the broken glass behind an old clock. While Mrs. Dreyer maintains the breakage was merely a result of her own clumsiness and fatigue, Poirot suspects she was actually frightened by something.

When Poirot asks if she saw anyone leaving the library, Mrs. Dreyer firmly denies seeing anyone. Poirot remains skeptical, suspecting that her determination to deny seeing anyone stems from a protective instinct to exonerate a young person she cares about. Dr. Drake suggests that Miss Whitaker might have seen someone entering the library shortly before the vase incident, potentially a child or a young boy, and that Mrs. Dreyer's reaction was an attempt to avoid being unfair to a person she only partially identified.
